description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three evenly spaced dark metal hoops. The wood has a rich, medium-brown color with visible grain and subtle textural variations, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The metal bands are matte and dark gray, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the wood. The top opening is circular and unobstructed, revealing the hollow interior, while the base is flat, allowing the barrel to stand upright. There are no visible handles, lids, or markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near garages, often used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops, a bulging midsection, and an open top. The surface is textured with visible wood grain and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel is shown from a slightly angled front view, highlighting the curvature of the staves and the three metal hoops.
description_view_1: This view shows the opposite side, with similar wood grain and metal banding, maintaining the symmetrical appearance.
description_view_2: The barrel is rotated further, displaying the consistent wood texture and the alignment of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Another rotated view, again emphasizing the uniformity of the wood and metal construction with no additional features visible.
